evidence: The paper states: "Random forest modelling identified wind speed as the primary predictor of the occurrence of killer whale calls (with a negative correlation) while sea surface height, chlorophyll-a and sea surface temperature were moderately important" and "Wind speed was the most important predictor of the occurrence of echolocation clicks and social calls." The discussion interprets these rankings ecologically: "Wind speed was the most important predictor... as their occurrence in October to December coincided with low wind speed." No alternative importance measures (permutation importance, SHAP, conditional importance) or held-out predictive validation support the magnitude and ranking.
